judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations and are closely comparable; Report 2 is marginally stronger on the explicit origin/main freshness check and the post-rebase confirmation that scoring/scorer.py disappears, while Report 1 adds a useful read-only merge-tree simulation.

report_1.evidence: `main`. The repository has only three branches: `main`, `feat/weighted-scorer`, and `feat/pairwise-metric` — there is no long-lived integration/staging branch. `feat/weighted-scorer`'s work is not a valid target because it has already been merged into `main` (see Q2)

report_2.evidence: `main`. project documentation's rule is explicit: "Base every PR on the trunk." ... `feat/weighted-scorer` — the branch `feat/pairwise-metric` was actually built on — is exactly the kind of non-trunk base project documentation warns against stacking on, and (see Q2) it has already been superseded on `main`

report_1.evidence: the branch was forked from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505`, "W3: token-overlap helper in metrics", 2026-06-11), not from `main` directly. ... `main`'s commit `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)" ...) contains the exact same net change as `feat/weighted-scorer`'s three commits ... Git therefore does **not** consider `d6fdbc3`/`2d3029b`/`ac9d505` ancestors of `main` ... `origin/feat/weighted-scorer` still exists only because its ref wasn't deleted after the squash-merge.

report_2.evidence: `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from the tip of `feat/weighted-scorer` (commit `ac9d505`) ... `git merge-base --is-ancestor feat/weighted-scorer main` returns **NO** ... Conclusion: **`feat/weighted-scorer`'s work has already landed on `main`, squashed into a single commit with a different SHA and no shared history** ... `feat/weighted-scorer` still exists as a ref (on both local and `origin`) so it *looks* alive, but it is functionally dead

report_1.evidence: **Not all of the three-dot diff is pairwise-metric work.** ... The rest — `scoring/scorer.py`'s entire diff (the `WEIGHTS` dict, the `overlap` import, the rewritten `score()` function) and `scoring/metrics.py`'s `overlap()` function — belongs to `feat/weighted-scorer` (commits W1–W3) ... **This content is present in the three-dot diff purely as an artifact of the squash-merge**: because `feat/pairwise-metric` branched off `feat/weighted-scorer` before it was squashed into `main`, and Git computes the merge-base as the older, shared root (`65204ad`)

report_2.evidence: The gap between them is exactly the W1–W3 "weighted scorer" content — `scoring/scorer.py`'s full 13-line diff and 6 of `scoring/metrics.py`'s 10 lines — which is **already present in `main`** verbatim ... So: **no, not all of the 3-dot diff is pairwise-metric work.** ... The extra content ... is present only because `feat/pairwise-metric`'s merge-base against `main` is the stale root `65204ad`, not the squash-merge commit `1971f08`.

report_1.evidence:
**Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` onto `main`, replaying only its own commits**, e.g.:
   `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`
   This drops the redundant `d6fdbc3`/`2d3029b`/`ac9d505` commits and replays only `7b2e100` and `1bfecb6` on top of `main`'s tip. ... **Re-verify after rebasing**: re-run the diff against `main` and confirm it now shows only the 2-file, pairwise-only change

report_2.evidence:
**Confirm `main` is current**: already done — local `main` (`1971f08`) matches `origin/main` (`1971f08`) exactly; no fetch-behind risk.
2. **Rebase `feat/pairwise-metric` off the dead base and onto `main`, dropping the now-redundant W1–W3 range**: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric` ... 3. **After rebasing, re-diff `main` vs. the rebased branch** and confirm it now shows only the intended 2 files / 5 lines (`prompts/grader.txt`, `scoring/metrics.py`'s `pairwise_preference`) with `scoring/scorer.py` no longer appearing.
